text:
I opened **9 GitHub issues** in the `acme/web` repository. 

These correspond to 9 merged pull requests across 4 repositories (`acme/web`, `acme/api`, `acme/mobile`, `acme/infra`) that close Linear issues. Each new issue has the title `Verify: ` followed by the original PR title:

| Issue # | Title |
|---------|-------|
| 2030 | Verify: Mobile crash on iOS 18 cold start: guard against ios18-crash |
| 2031 | Verify: Mobile crash on iOS 18 cold start: guard against ios18-crash |
| 2032 | Verify: Mobile crash on iOS 18 cold start: fix ios18-crash |
| 2033 | Verify: Search relevance regression: fix search-relevance |
| 2034 | Verify: Rate limiter false positives: add test for rate-limiter |
| 2035 | Verify: Primary DB failover is slow: guard against db-failover |
| 2036 | Verify: Migrate auth to OAuth2 + PKCE: fix oauth-migration |
| 2037 | Verify: Image uploads time out over 5MB: add test for image-uploads |
| 2038 | Verify: SSO SCIM provisioning errors: refactor sso-provisioning |

merged_prs = github.list_pull_requests(state="merged")

# Check what we got
len(merged_prs), merged_prs[:3] if merged_prs else "No merged PRs""}

tool_calls.name: execute_python
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"# Create issues in acme/web for each PR that closes a Linear issue
created_issues = []
for pr in closing_linear_prs:
    issue_title = f"Verify: {pr['title']}"
    issue = github.create_issue(repo="acme/web", title=issue_title)
    created_issues.append(issue)

len(created_issues)"}

tool_calls.name: execute_python
tool_calls.input: {"code":"# Summary of created issues\n[(issue[\"number\"], issue[\"title\"]) for issue in created_issues]"}
